Narrative: (1) Continuation of any preceding items. (2) Property Listing, to include property taken and arizedi submited mundenon/property; ilet property inventory number (s) when applicable. On 3/22/17 at 0412 hours this officer was dispatched to 2400 Guilford for a shooting. Upon responding this officer located a b/m later identified as Alphonza Watson
laying on the sidewalk in front of 2400 Guilford on his back with his feet in the street. Mr. Watson was holding his right side and complaining of chest pain and difficulty breathing from an apparent gunshot wound. Medic #16 responded to location and transported Mr. Watson to Johns Hopkins where at 0544 hours Mr. Watson succumbed to his wounds. Dr. Efron pronounced at 0544 hours. Investigation revealed Mr Watson was in the 2400 block of Guilford Ave where he was shot by unknown male who fled the area.

Upon arriving Mr. Alphonza Watson was suffering a gunshot would to his right chest/ stomach area. Mr. Watson was laying on his back on the sidewalk with his head facing west and his feet in the street facing cast. Medic # 16 responded and transported Mr Watson to : Johns Hopkins where he was immediately taken to the operating room. At 0544 hours Dr Efron pronounced Mr Watson deceased. City wide shooting responded out still investigating this incident as a shooting. Detective Fuller H089 Unit 3845 on scene. Crime Jab Harden Unit 5835 on scene to photograph and recover evidence. Crime scene log maintained by Officer Chanoine J294 3A42. Unit 5A42 Officer Moorehead followed Medic to Johns Hopkins and recovered and submitted all evidence while at the hospital. Upon being made aware that Mr. Watson was deceased investigation was turned over to Homicide Detective Jackson Unit 6486

laying on the sidewalk in front of 2400 Guilford on his back with his feet in the street. Mr. Watson was holding his right side and complaining of chest pain and difficulty breathing from an apparent gunshot wound. Upon arriving Mr. Alphonza Watson was suffering a gunshot would to his right chest/ stomach area. Mr. Watson was laying on his back on the sidewalk with his head facing west and his feet in the street facing cast. Medic # 16 responded and transported Mr Watson to : Johns Hopkins where he was immediately taken to the operating room. At 0544 hours Dr Efron pronounced Mr Watson deceased. City wide shooting responded out still investigating this incident as a shooting. Detective Fuller H089 Unit 3845 on scene. Crime Jab Harden Unit 5835 on scene to photograph and recover evidence. Crime scene log maintained by Officer Chanoine J294 3A42. Unit 5A42 Officer Moorehead followed Medic to Johns Hopkins and recovered and submitted all evidence while at the hospital.
